summary: User asks for a subjective rating from an LLM. The composer_hints on the llm transform tell the LLM operating the composer (1) to verify the model via list_models before pinning, and (2) to trigger request_interpretation_review when the prompt contains subjective terms. Hint-blind LLMs hardcode an unverified model name and skip interpretation review — the run ships with an opaque-to-the-operator interpretation of 'cool'. Green-passes when the LLM either invoked list_models OR triggered interpretation review (the post_call_hint also fires automatically because the prompt contains 'cool'). Authored 2026-05-20 for composer-jit-hints Phase 1.
opening_prompt:
Rate how cool each of these government websites is, on a 1-5 scale. Use a fast LLM. Write the rated rows to outputs/rated.jsonl.

https://dta.gov.au
https://www.digital.nsw.gov.au
https://www.vic.gov.au/digital-strategy
https://www.qld.gov.au/digital

build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.must_be_valid_note: Pipeline must be valid before this scenario can score anything meaningful.

green_criteria.must_have_node_kinds_note: The pipeline must contain an llm transform — without it, the rating step never happens.
green_criteria._interpretation_review_note: The llm transform's get_post_call_hints fires on subjective prompt terms like 'cool'; the LLM should respond by triggering request_interpretation_review. Detecting that call programmatically requires inspecting the tool-call message stream, which the persisted chat history does not preserve. The is_valid + llm-node-present asserts pin the structural floor; reviewer reads the final assistant content to confirm interpretation acknowledgement.
